resources.string: General tweaks that affect the whole app.
Controls parallel operations when scanning apps. Higher values are faster but may cause crashes on some devices.
A place to hang out in and ask questions.
A public issue tracker for bug reports and feature requests.
Frequently asked questions and common solutions.
For supporting translation of open-source projects.
Unknown permission. It is requested by this app, but no other app declares it.
Unknown permission. It is requested by apps, but not declared anywhere.
Permission is created by this app.
Could not open app settings for this profile

string-array.name: generic_loading_labels
string-array.item: The CPU is on a coffee break

resources.string: Record everything the app is doing into a text file that you can share.
No action available for this permission
Chips show which sensitive permission categories an app has been granted
This app is installed in another user profile. Permissions granted can be determined only for apps installed in the currently Active Profile, due to Android restrictions.
This app has been uninstalled but its data has been retained. Permission states cannot be determined for uninstalled apps.
No permissions match current filters. Tap the filter icon to adjust.
Runtime — The app must ask you before using this permission. You can grant or deny it.
Special — Requires toggling a switch in system settings.
Install — Granted automatically when the app is installed. No user action needed.
The ⚠ icon means this permission is created by the app itself, rather than being a standard Android permission.
Allows the app to reboot the device. Only available to system apps.

plurals.name: permissions_details_count_user_apps
plurals.item: User Apps: Granted to %1$d out of %2$d.
User Apps: Granted to %1$d out of %2$d.

plurals.name: permissions_details_count_system_apps
plurals.item: System Apps: Granted to %1$d out of %2$d.
System Apps: Granted to %1$d out of %2$d.

plurals.name: permissions_details_count_other_profile_apps
plurals.item: %d app in other user profiles also requests this permission.
%d apps in other user profiles also request this permission.

plurals.name: permissions_details_count_uninstalled_apps
plurals.item: %d uninstalled app with retained data also requests this permission.
%d uninstalled apps with retained data also request this permission.

resources.string: Apps installed in other user profiles are excluded from the computation above due to Android restrictions.
No apps match current filters. Tap the filter icon to adjust.
Dangerous — Requires explicit user approval at runtime. Can access sensitive data or device features.
Normal — Granted automatically at install. Poses minimal risk to other apps or the system.
Signature — Only granted to apps signed with the same certificate as the app that declared the permission.
Internal — Reserved for Android system internals. Not available to third-party apps.
Runtime — The app must ask you before using this permission.
Install-time — Granted automatically when the app is installed.
Special access — Requires toggling a switch in system settings.
Documented — Listed in the official Android manifest documentation.
Notable — Particularly noteworthy due to its impact on privacy or security.
Non-standard — Not part of the standard Android permission set. Declared by a third-party app or OEM.
Granted — The app has been given this permission.
In use — Granted and currently being actively used by the app.
Denied — The app requested this permission but it was not granted.
Unknown — The grant state cannot be determined, e.g. for apps in other user profiles.
System — The app is a pre-installed system app.
Allows apps to communicate with Near Field Communication (NFC) tags, cards and readers.
Allows apps to modify or delete media files created with other apps WITHOUT asking you. Apps must have permission to access files and media.
Allows apps to track what other apps you use and how often, identify your service provider, language settings and other usage data.
Allows apps to modify system\'s settings data. Malicious apps may corrupt your system\'s configuration.
Allows apps to advertise to nearby Bluetooth devices.
Allows apps to get the list of accounts known by the device. This may include any accounts created by applications that you have installed.
Allows apps to access location any time, even when they are not in use.
Allows apps to read location from your media collection.
Allows apps to manage accounts in the device.
Allows apps to turn WiFi ON or OFF, scan and connect to WiFi networks, add or remove networks, or start a local-only HotSpot.
Allows apps to change the state of network connectivity.
Allows apps to read sync settings for an account.
Allows apps to manually install other apps on your device. This may put your device and data at risk.

Allows apps to read, modify and delete all files on your device as well as any connected storage devices. Apps with this permission may also access files that aren\'t related to the app, WITHOUT notifying you.
Allows apps to show things on top of other apps you\'re using (including showing ads). This may interfere with your use of other apps.
Allows apps access to files and media on your device.
Allows apps access to media only on your device.

Allows apps to access your device\'s accurate/ precise location. This is NOT recommended for most apps (except for some apps like maps, cab services, device tracking services like Find My Device etc.).
Allows apps to access your device\'s approximate location. This permission is sufficient for most apps that need access to your location for core functionality.

Allows apps to run unhindered in the background. This may drain battery. Recommended only for apps that perform continuous/ frequent backups and/ or syncs (e.g. Google Photos), very frequently used apps (e.g. email, chat, sms, etc.), as well as for those apps that have a persistent notification.

Allows apps to read all your notifications, including personal information (such as contact names and text of messages you receive), snooze or dismiss notifications or trigger action buttons they contain (such as answer phone calls, delete your sms/ email, etc) and turn Do Not Disturb (DND) ON or OFF and change related settings.
Allows apps to turn Do Not Disturb (DND) ON or OFF and change related settings.

Allows apps to set alarms and schedule time-sensitive actions. This allows apps to run in the background, which may use more battery. Turning OFF this permission for an app would cause existing alarms and time-based events that are scheduled by that particular app to stop working.
Allows apps to turn on the device screen when they need your attention, such as for alarms or incoming calls.
Allows apps to show full-screen notifications that take over your screen when the device is locked or in use. Commonly used for alarms, calls, and other time-sensitive events.
Allows apps to communicate between your work and personal profiles, sharing data and functionality across profile boundaries.
Allows apps to collect statistics about data loader usage on the device.
Allows apps to use premium text message services. This will cost you money.
Allows apps access to unrestricted data. Apps can consume data even when Data Saver is ON.
Calendar related permissions, e.g. view & edit entries.
File related permissions. Access to storage, documents and media.
SMS, MMS and more. Everything messaging related.
WiFi, LTE, Bluetooth and more. Permissions related to communicating with the outside world.
Accelerometer, Gyro, Body Sensors, Activity Recognition, and more. Ways that your device can capture input.
Special Access permissions requiring explicit user consent.
Allows apps to access external storage without scoped storage restrictions. This was the default behavior before Android 10. Apps with this access can read and write files anywhere on shared storage.
Apps in Managed User Profile(s), excluding Clones
Apps manually installed from external sources
Apps with \'Install from Unknown Sources\' enabled
Apps with clones in other profiles
Allows the app to offer an accessibility service that can be enabled in the system settings.
This file contains sensitive information (e.g. your installed applications). Only share it with trusted parties.

This feature records everything the app is doing into a shareable file. The generated file contains sensitive information (e.g. your installed applications). Only share it with trusted parties (e.g. the app developer if you are troubleshooting an issue together).
Fill out a form to send the developer an email.
A debug log needs to capture the issue while it happens. Start the recording, reproduce your issue, then stop the recording.
This will delete all stored debug log files.
This debug log will be permanently deleted.
If you sent the debug log, it can be deleted to free up space.
If you sent the email, this session can be cleaned up.
Describe your question in detail. Please be specific (minimum 20 words).
Describe the feature you would like to see. Please be specific (minimum 20 words).
Describe what happened and how to reproduce the issue. Please be specific (minimum 20 words).
Describe what you expected to happen (minimum 10 words).
Attach an existing debug log, or record a new one.
No debug logs found. Record one first.
This debug log will be permanently deleted.
No email app found on this device.
I read every message myself and do my best to reply. Since I handle development, bug fixes, and support on my own, it can take a little time. Thanks for your patience.
Your message will be sent via email. Device and setup information is attached automatically. You can attach screenshots or a video to the email. Please write in English if possible.
This app helps you navigate installed applications and discover permissions.
Permission Pilot collects Installed Application information to enable listing your apps and cross-referencing their permissions when using this application.
Permission Pilot has no ads and only processes data locally.
Get notified when an app update adds new permissions or changes existing ones. Permission Watcher checks your apps after each update and creates a report if anything changed.
Can be disabled and configured in Settings
Notifications about permission changes in installed apps
Detect permission changes when apps are installed or updated
Show a notification when permission changes are detected
Only notify when apps are granted new permissions, e.g. a runtime permission is allowed or an install-time permission like internet is added
All permission change reports will be permanently deleted.
Permission changes are being tracked, but notifications can\'t be shown. Enable notifications to receive alerts about permission changes.
These permissions were added with this app version.
These permissions were removed in this update.
These permissions had their grant status changed.
These were the permissions the app had before removal.
No reports match your search or filters
Upgrade to view all reports, details, and get notifications
List apps that request each permission
Free version is limited to %d items and Markdown format. Upgrade for unlimited exports and all formats.
